summary: Comprehensive testing of Class Series feature completed. Backend: 18/18 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All critical flows verified - Classes CRUD, Schedule-Class linkage, Calendar/Kanban class display, Workload/Report class filtering.

issues: Some schedules show 'Unassigned Class' - this is expected for schedules created before class feature or without class assignment

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/classes - returns list of classes
Backend: POST /api/classes - creates class with name, description, color
Backend: PUT /api/classes/{id} - updates class and syncs to linked schedules
Backend: DELETE /api/classes/{id} - deletes class, preserves schedule data with class_name
Backend: POST /api/schedules with class_id - creates schedule with class linkage
Backend: GET /api/schedules - returns schedules with class_name, class_color
Backend: Class update syncs class_name/class_color to all linked schedules
Backend: Class deletion preserves class_name in schedule (class_id becomes null)
Backend: GET /api/workload - returns class_breakdown per employee
Backend: GET /api/reports/weekly-summary - returns class_totals and employee class_breakdown
Backend: GET /api/reports/weekly-summary with class_id filter works
Frontend: Sidebar has Classes navigation item
Frontend: ClassManager component loads with Add Class button
Frontend: Add Class dialog opens with name, description, color fields
Frontend: Class creation works and shows success toast
Frontend: Schedule form has Class Type selector
Frontend: Schedule form has inline 'Add New Class' button
Frontend: ClassQuickCreateDialog opens from schedule form
Frontend: Calendar view shows class blocks with colors
Frontend: Kanban board shows class names with colors on cards
Frontend: Workload Dashboard has class filter dropdown
Frontend: Workload Dashboard shows class chips
Frontend: Weekly Report has class filter dropdown
Frontend: Weekly Report shows class breakdown per employee
critical_code_review_comments: Code quality is good - proper separation of concerns between ClassManager, ClassQuickCreateDialog, and ScheduleForm
Backend properly handles class deletion by preserving class_name in schedules
sync_class_snapshot function correctly updates all linked schedules when class is modified
seed_data_creation: Test users created during testing: test_ui_*, verify_* prefixed emails
context_for_next_testing_agent: Class Series feature fully implemented and tested. All CRUD operations work. Schedule-Class linkage persists correctly. Class updates sync to schedules. Class deletion preserves readable schedule data. UI components (ClassManager, ClassQuickCreateDialog, ScheduleForm class selector) all functional. Workload and Weekly Report class filtering/grouping works.

features_verified.sidebar_classes_nav: PASS - Classes navigation item present and functional
features_verified.schedule_class_selector: PASS - Class Type dropdown in schedule form
features_verified.inline_class_creation: PASS - Add New Class button opens quick create dialog
features_verified.calendar_class_display: PASS - Class blocks show class_name and class_color
features_verified.kanban_class_display: PASS - Cards show class name badges with colors
features_verified.workload_class_filter: PASS - Filter by Class dropdown functional
features_verified.weekly_report_class_filter: PASS - Filter by Class dropdown functional
features_verified.class_update_sync: PASS - Updating class syncs to all linked schedules
features_verified.class_delete_preserves_data: PASS - Deleting class preserves class_name in schedules
